title: Health and the body
description: A2 vocabulary and grammar for talking about health: body parts, the construction avoir mal à (with contractions au/aux), common symptoms, visiting the doctor, and giving advice with tu devrais and il faut. Explained in English.

cards.front: la tête, la gorge, le ventre, le dos, les jambes, les dents
cards.back: the head, the throat, the stomach/belly, the back, the legs, the teeth
cards.notes: Key body parts you need for describing pain. Note the genders: **la** tête (f), **la** gorge (f), **le** ventre (m), **le** dos (m), **les** jambes (f pl), **les** dents (f pl).

cards.back: to have pain in / to hurt (a body part)
cards.notes:
The standard French construction for body pain. Literally *to have pain at*. You MUST use the contracted article:

| Article needed | Contracted form | Example |
|---|---|---|
| à + **la** | **à la** (no change) | *j'ai mal **à la** tête* |
| à + **le** | **au** | *j'ai mal **au** dos* |
| à + **les** | **aux** | *j'ai mal **aux** dents* |

**English trap:** 'I have a headache' = *j'ai mal à la tête* — do NOT say *j'ai un mal de tête* (that is wrong at A2).
cards.hint: à + le = au; à + les = aux; à + la stays à la.

cards.front: j'ai mal à la tête / j'ai mal au dos / j'ai mal aux dents
cards.back: I have a headache / my back hurts / my teeth hurt
cards.notes: *à la tête* — feminine singular, no contraction needed. *au dos* — masculine singular: à + le → **au**. *aux dents* — plural: à + les → **aux**. These three sentences cover all three contraction cases.

cards.front: j'ai de la fièvre / je tousse / j'ai un rhume / je suis fatigué(e) / j'ai mal au cœur
cards.back: I have a fever / I am coughing / I have a cold / I am tired / I feel nauseous (lit. I have pain at the heart)
cards.notes:
Common symptoms:
- *j'ai **de la** fièvre* — fever (partitive with fièvre)
- *je **tousse*** — I cough (tousser, regular -er)
- *j'ai **un rhume*** — a cold
- *je suis **fatigué(e)*** — tired (adjective agrees with gender)
- *j'ai mal **au cœur*** — nauseous; literally 'I have pain at the heart' — a classic false friend. Do NOT translate literally into English.
cards.hint: j'ai mal au coeur = I feel sick/nauseous, NOT a heart problem.

cards.front: prendre rendez-vous / le médecin / une ordonnance / un médicament / la pharmacie
cards.back: to make an appointment / the doctor / a prescription / a medicine / the pharmacy
cards.notes:
Useful phrases for a medical visit:
- *prendre rendez-vous* — to make an appointment (lit. to take a meeting)
- *le médecin* — the doctor (gender-neutral noun, always **le**)
- *une ordonnance* — a prescription
- *un médicament* — a medicine/medication
- *la pharmacie* — the pharmacy

cards.front: tu devrais te reposer / il faut boire de l'eau / repose-toi
cards.back: you should rest / you must/one must drink water / rest! (command)
cards.notes:
Three ways to give advice:

| Structure | Example | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **tu devrais** + infinitive | *tu devrais **te reposer*** | polite advice, conditional of devoir |
| **il faut** + infinitive | *il faut **boire de l'eau*** | impersonal 'one must / you need to' |
| **imperative** | ***repose-toi*** | direct command; reflexive verbs add -toi |

*Devoir* (to have to/must) in the conditional: *je devrais, tu devrais, il devrait, nous devrions, vous devriez, ils devraient.*
cards.hint: tu devrais = conditional of devoir (you should); il faut = it is necessary (impersonal).

cards.notes: *Un rhume* is a common cold. Do not confuse with *la grippe* (the flu). Both use **avoir**: *j'ai un rhume*, *j'ai la grippe*.
*Fièvre* is a feminine noun and takes the **partitive** article *de la*: *j'ai **de la** fièvre*. English says 'a fever' with the indefinite article, but French uses the partitive here.
*Une ordonnance* is what a doctor writes for you to get medicine at the pharmacy. The verb to use: *le médecin m'a donné une ordonnance* (the doctor gave me a prescription).
*Tousser* is a regular **-er** verb: *je tousse, tu tousses, il/elle tousse, nous toussons, vous toussez, ils/elles toussent*.

cards.back: I feel nauseous / I feel sick to my stomach
cards.notes: Literally 'I have pain at the heart' — but in French this means **nausea**, not a heart problem. A classic false friend. For actual heart pain, French says *j'ai des douleurs cardiaques*.
cards.hint: mal au coeur = nausea, NOT a heart attack.

steps.title: Body parts and avoir mal à
steps.body:
# Health and the body

To talk about pain in French, the key construction is:

> **avoir mal à** + (contracted article) + body part

This literally means 'to have pain at'. The article after **à** must contract:

| Article | Contracts to | Example |
|---|---|---|
| à + **la** | **à la** | *j'ai mal **à la** gorge* (I have a sore throat) |
| à + **le** | **au** | *j'ai mal **au** ventre* (my stomach hurts) |
| à + **les** | **aux** | *j'ai mal **aux** jambes* (my legs hurt) |

## Common body parts

| French | English | Gender |
|---|---|---|
| la tête | the head | f |
| la gorge | the throat | f |
| le ventre | the stomach/belly | m |
| le dos | the back | m |
| les jambes | the legs | f pl |
| les dents | the teeth | f pl |

## English speaker note

'I have a headache' = **j'ai mal à la tête** (NOT *j'ai un mal de tête*). French uses *avoir mal à* where English uses 'have a -ache'.

steps.title: Symptoms and seeing the doctor
steps.body:
## Common symptoms

| French | English | Note |
|---|---|---|
| *j'ai de la fièvre* | I have a fever | partitive *de la* with fièvre |
| *je tousse* | I am coughing | regular -er verb |
| *j'ai un rhume* | I have a cold | indefinite article |
| *je suis fatigué(e)* | I am tired | adjective — add -e if female |
| *j'ai mal au cœur* | I feel nauseous | **false friend** — not a heart problem! |

## At the doctor

| French | English |
|---|---|
| prendre rendez-vous | to make an appointment |
| le médecin | the doctor |
| une ordonnance | a prescription |
| un médicament | a medicine |
| la pharmacie | the pharmacy |

steps.title: Giving advice about health
steps.body:
## Three ways to give advice in French

| Structure | Example | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| **tu devrais** + infinitive | *tu devrais **te reposer*** | you should rest (conditional, polite) |
| **il faut** + infinitive | *il faut **prendre un médicament*** | you must take medicine (impersonal) |
| **imperative** | ***repose-toi!*** | rest! (direct command) |

### Tu devrais — conditional of devoir

*Devoir* in the conditional expresses 'should/ought to'. For advice use **tu devrais** + infinitive:
- *Tu devrais **aller** chez le médecin.* (You should go to the doctor.)
- *Tu devrais **boire** beaucoup d'eau.* (You should drink lots of water.)

### Il faut + infinitive

Impersonal and neutral — 'one must / it is necessary to':
- *Il faut **se reposer**.* (You need to rest.)
- *Il faut **prendre** cette ordonnance à la pharmacie.* (You need to take this prescription to the pharmacy.)

### Repose-toi — imperative of reflexive verbs

In the imperative, reflexive verbs use **-toi** (informal you): ***repose-toi*** (rest!), ***lève-toi*** (get up!).

steps.title: Match the symptom to its meaning

exercise.prompt: Match each French expression to its English meaning.
Translate: *I have a headache.* Use the avoir mal à construction with the correct contracted article.
exercise.hint: tête is feminine (la tête), so: à + la = à la. No contraction needed.

steps.title: Fill in the contraction: back pain

exercise.prompt: Fill in the correct contracted form of 'à + le' for *dos* (masculine).
exercise.sentence: Depuis ce matin, j'ai mal ___ dos.
exercise.hint: dos is masculine (le dos): à + le = au.

exercise.prompt: Arrange the words to say: *You should rest.*
exercise.hint: tu devrais + infinitive of the reflexive verb se reposer.

steps.title: My teeth hurt — which sentence is correct?

exercise.prompt: Someone is pointing to their teeth and saying 'My teeth hurt.' Which sentence is correct?
exercise.hint: dents is plural (les dents): à + les = aux.

steps.title: Translate: I have a sore throat

exercise.prompt: Translate: *I have a sore throat.* (gorge is feminine — la gorge)
exercise.hint: gorge is feminine (la gorge): à + la stays à la.

exercise.prompt: Match each French term to its English meaning.
Complete the advice sentence with the correct impersonal form meaning 'it is necessary to'.
exercise.sentence: ___ prendre ce médicament trois fois par jour.
exercise.hint: The impersonal advice structure is il faut + infinitive.

steps.title: Give advice: you should see the doctor

exercise.prompt: Translate: *You should see the doctor.* Use **tu devrais** + aller chez le médecin.
accept: Tu devrais aller chez le médecin.
tu devrais aller chez le médecin.
tu devrais aller chez le médecin
Tu devrais aller chez le médecin
exercise.hint: tu devrais (conditional of devoir) + infinitive: aller chez le médecin.
distractors: Tu dois aller chez le médecin.
Tu devrais vas chez le médecin.
Il faut tu aller chez le médecin.

steps.title: Build: I have a fever and I am coughing

exercise.prompt: Arrange the words to say: *I have a fever and I am coughing.*
exercise.hint: j'ai de la fièvre (partitive) + et + je tousse.
